data.title: Franklin Templeton Turns Treasury Funds Into Stablecoin Reserve Infrastructure
data.description:
Franklin Templeton has updated two of its institutional money market funds to directly support the stablecoin and tokenized finance ecosystem, aligning them with the U. S. federal stablecoin framework. The changes, announced on January 13, 2026, position the funds to act as regulated reserve vehicles for stablecoins while remaining fully compliant with existing U
source: https://www.ethnews.com/franklin-templeton-turns-treasury-funds-into-stablecoin-reserve-infrastructure/

data.title: Crypto Investor’s Family Tied Up and Beaten by Armed Gangs in Their Home
data.description:
Three armed men invaded a crypto investor's home in Verneuil-sur-Seine, France, binding and beating the family before fleeing, as France leads global crypto-related violence with 14 documented attacks following revelations that criminals exploit government tax databases to identify and target cryptocurrency holders.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/crypto-investors-family-tied-up-and-beaten-by-armed-gangs-in-their-home/

data.title: Telenor Sells Pakistani Unit to PTCL
data.description:
Telenor has completed the sale of its Pakistani unit to PTCL, exiting the market and enabling the merged entity to become Pakistan’s second-largest mobile operator.
source: https://catenaa.com/industries/telecom/telenor-sells-pakistani-unit-to-ptcl/

data.title: South Korea’s Korbit fined $2 million for money laundering
data.description:
The South Korean crypto exchange Korbit has accepted a fine of nearly $2 million. The exchange also has an official warning for widespread failures in its AML and customer verification systems. In a statement, Korbit stated, “We respectfully and humbly accept the Financial Intelligence Unit’s decision to impose a fine. […] Despite the difficult circumstances, […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/korbit-fined-2-million-for-money-laundering/

data.title: CZ Warns Traders About Meme Coins Tied to His Tweets — Here Is Why He’s Urging Caution
data.description:
CZ, the CEO of Binance, has issued a stern warning to traders regarding the potential risks associated with investing in meme coins that are linked to his tweets. He emphasizes the importance of exercising caution and due diligence before jumping into these volatile assets. In his statement, he outlines several reasons for this advisory, highlighting the unpredictable nature of meme coins, their tendency for rapid price fluctuations, and the influence that social media can have on market sentiment. CZ believes that while meme coins may seem appealing due to their explosive growth potential, investors should be wary of the risks involved, especially when making decisions based solely on social media activity. He urges traders to conduct thorough research and consider the fundamentals of any cryptocurrency before investing.
source: https://blocknews.com/cz-warns-traders-about-meme-coins-tied-to-his-tweets-here-is-why-hes-urging-caution/

data.title: Former New York Mayor’s NYC memecoin rugpulls investors at launch, and the method used was shockingly brazen
data.description:
Former New York mayor Eric Adams’ new NYC token fell more than 81% within about 30 minutes of its Jan. 12 launch on Solana, wiping out roughly $500 million in peak paper value. The token briefly reached an estimated market cap of $540 million–$600 million
source: https://cryptoslate.com/eric-adams-nyc-token-crashed-81-and-a-single-wallets-massive-70-holding-reveals-why-the-game-was-rigged/

data.title: NFT Paris 2026 cancelled: NFT crisis and fear on the Web3
data.description:
NFT Paris and RWA Paris 2026 are cancelled a month early. Amidst the collapse of the NFT market, fleeing sponsors and growing fears for personal safety, Europe's flagship Web3 event closes a historic chapter.
source: https://en.spaziocrypto.com/nft/nft-paris-2026-cancelled-nft-crisis-and-fear-on-the-web3/

data.title: Nigeria set to lead Africa with first economy-wide AI regulations
data.description:
Nigeria is preparing to approve legislation that will put the country at the forefront of African nations working to control artificial intelligence, marking a shift after tech companies operated with little supervision for years in one of the continent’s largest digital economies. The National Digital Economy and E-Governance Bill will hand regulators authority over data, […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/nigeria-set-to-lead-africa-with-first-economy-wide-ai-regulations/
